Package global.thalion.ttio.providers

Class Hdf5Provider

java.lang.Object
global.thalion.ttio.providers.Hdf5Provider
All Implemented Interfaces:
StorageProvider, AutoCloseable
public final class Hdf5Provider extends Object implements StorageProvider
HDF5 storage provider. Adapter over the existing Hdf5File / Hdf5Group / Hdf5Dataset layer — no behavioural change; callers that used those directly can switch to Hdf5Provider.open(path, Mode.READ) and continue.

API status: Stable (Provisional per M39 — may change before v1.0).

Cross-language equivalents:

  • Objective-C: TTIOHDF5Provider
  • Python: ttio.providers.hdf5.Hdf5Provider
Since:
0.6

  • Method Details

    • providerName

      public String providerName()
      Description copied from interface: StorageProvider
      Short identifier used for logging and registry lookup.
      Specified by:
      providerName in interface StorageProvider

    • supportsUrl

      public boolean supportsUrl(String pathOrUrl)
      Description copied from interface: StorageProvider
      Return true if this provider supports opening the given path or URL. Used by the registry to pick a provider by scheme when the caller hasn't named one explicitly.
      Specified by:
      supportsUrl in interface StorageProvider

    • open

      public StorageProvider open(String pathOrUrl, StorageProvider.Mode mode)
      Description copied from interface: StorageProvider
      Open the backing store. The provider instance is returned so chaining is possible: new Hdf5Provider().open(...).

      Re-opening an already-open provider is an error.

      Specified by:
      open in interface StorageProvider

    • rootGroup

      public StorageGroup rootGroup()
      Description copied from interface: StorageProvider
      Root group ("/"). Must be called after StorageProvider.open(java.lang.String, global.thalion.ttio.providers.StorageProvider.Mode).
      Specified by:
      rootGroup in interface StorageProvider

    • adapterForGroup

      public static StorageGroup adapterForGroup(Hdf5Group group)
      v0.7 M44: wrap a raw Hdf5Group in the provider adapter so callers holding the low-level handle (AcquisitionRun, SpectralDataset) can hand it off as a protocol StorageGroup. The adapter does not take ownership of the underlying HDF5 handle; the caller must keep the Hdf5Group alive for as long as the adapter is used.
      Since:
      0.7

    • adapterForDataset

      public static StorageDataset adapterForDataset(Hdf5Dataset dataset, String name)
      v0.7 M44: wrap a raw Hdf5Dataset as a protocol StorageDataset. Same non-owning semantics as adapterForGroup(global.thalion.ttio.hdf5.Hdf5Group).
      Since:
      0.7

    • isOpen

      public boolean isOpen()
      Specified by:
      isOpen in interface StorageProvider

    • nativeHandle

      public Object nativeHandle()
      Description copied from interface: StorageProvider
      Return the underlying native storage handle — an Hdf5File for Hdf5Provider, null for MemoryProvider.

      Escape hatch for byte-level code (signatures, encryption, native compression filters) that cannot be expressed through the protocol. Any caller that invokes this is pinned to a specific backend.

      Specified by:
      nativeHandle in interface StorageProvider

    • supportsChunking

      public boolean supportsChunking()
      Description copied from interface: StorageProvider
      true if the backend honors chunkSize in StorageGroup.createDataset(java.lang.String, global.thalion.ttio.Enums.Precision, long, int, global.thalion.ttio.Enums.Compression, int). Defaults to false — only Hdf5Provider returns true. Memory and SQLite accept chunkSize for interface compatibility but silently ignore it.
      Specified by:
      supportsChunking in interface StorageProvider

    • supportsCompression

      public boolean supportsCompression()
      Description copied from interface: StorageProvider
      true if the backend honors compression / compressionLevel. Defaults to false. Only Hdf5Provider returns true (zlib + LZ4).
      Specified by:
      supportsCompression in interface StorageProvider

    • close

      public void close()
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
      Specified by:
      close in interface StorageProvider
